Class CharacterSelectionScene

java.lang.Object
gui.CharacterSelectionScene
public class CharacterSelectionScene extends Object
The CharacterSelectionScene class renders the team selection screen.

This scene allows the player to:

  • View available heroes from GameEngine.getAvailableHeroes()
  • Select and deselect heroes into the team (up to 3 members)
  • View hero preview information (name, stats label, skill, and ultimate description)
  • Proceed to the next scene when the team is complete

Selection behavior:

  • Clicking a hero portrait toggles an information view for that hero
  • Only one hero information view can be expanded at a time
  • Clicking the "Choose" button toggles hero membership in the team

This class is designed as a static scene utility with helper methods for building interactive image-based buttons.

  • Field Details

    • resetCurrentSelection

      private static Runnable resetCurrentSelection
      A callback used to reset the currently expanded hero portrait, ensuring that only one hero information panel is expanded at a time.

    • discription

      private static String[][] discription
      Static text data used to display hero details in the portrait scroll overlay.

      Each row corresponds to one hero, and columns are:

      • 0: hero name
      • 1: stats placeholder text
      • 2: skill name label
      • 3: skill description
      • 4: ultimate name label
      • 5: ultimate description

      Note: values are currently placeholders for HP, ATK, and DEF.

  • Constructor Details

    • CharacterSelectionScene

      public CharacterSelectionScene()

  • Method Details

    • show

      public static void show(javafx.stage.Stage stage, GameEngine gameEngine)
      Displays the character selection scene on the provided stage.

      This method:

      • Sets the game state to GameState.SELECT_TEAM
      • Builds the background and main layout
      • Creates hero portrait buttons and "Choose" buttons for team selection
      • Creates a start button that proceeds only when the team is full
      Parameters:
      stage - the JavaFX stage where this scene is shown
      gameEngine - the main game engine instance used for navigation and state

    • startBtnOnClickHandler

      private static void startBtnOnClickHandler(javafx.stage.Stage stage, GameEngine gameEngine)
      Handles the Start button click.

      If the team is full (exactly 3 heroes), this proceeds to the roll element scene. Otherwise, an error alert is shown to the player.

      Parameters:
      stage - the current JavaFX stage
      gameEngine - the game engine used to validate team size and navigate to the next scene

    • pickBtnOnClickHandler

      private static void pickBtnOnClickHandler(GameEngine gameEngine, Heroes hero, javafx.scene.control.Button pickBtn)
      Handles the Choose or Discard button behavior for a specific hero.

      This method toggles hero membership in the team via GameEngine.toggleTeamMember(Heroes) and updates the button graphic accordingly.

      If adding the hero would exceed the maximum team size, an error alert is shown and the selection change is not applied.

      Parameters:
      gameEngine - the game engine used to manage team membership
      hero - the hero to be toggled in or out of the team
      pickBtn - the button whose image is updated to reflect current selection state

    • createCharacterButton

      public static javafx.scene.control.Button createCharacterButton(String imagePath1, String imagePath2, int index)
      Creates a clickable hero portrait button that can expand into an information panel.

      Behavior:

      • Hover: temporarily swaps to an attack image if the button is not selected
      • Click: toggles selection and shows a scroll overlay containing hero descriptions
      • Only one hero button can be expanded at a time using resetCurrentSelection

      The expanded view uses a scroll image as background and overlays text from discription using the provided index.

      Parameters:
      imagePath1 - resource path for the hero idle image
      imagePath2 - resource path for the hero attack image
      index - index used to select text from discription
      Returns:
      a configured JavaFX Button representing a hero portrait

    • createButton

      private static javafx.scene.control.Button createButton(String path)
      Creates a generic image-based button used in the selection UI.

      This button supports:

      • Hover glow with scale-up animation
      • Press and release translate animation to simulate a click
      Parameters:
      path - resource path to the button image
      Returns:
      a styled JavaFX Button with hover and press animations
